label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Andrew could have gone to the clothing store. So the problem is asking between what times he could have gone. The key here is to look at all the times he was seen doing other things and figure out when he wasn't occupied, right? First, let me list out all the known activities and their times. Andrew woke up at 5am. Then, William saw him playing tennis from 5am to 6am. So from 5 to 6, he's playing tennis. Then Jessica saw him buying lunch at the deli from 6am to 9am. Wait, that's 6 to 9. Then Tiffany saw him taking photos near the Leaning Tower of Pisa from 9am to 11am. So 9 to 11. Then John saw him waiting at the train station from 11am to 5pm. That's a long time, 11am to 5pm. Then Sarah saw him reading at the library from 6pm to 9pm. And the clothing store was closed after 9pm. So now, we need to find the time intervals where Andrew wasn't doing any of these activities. Let me map out the timeline: - 5am to 6am: tennis - 6am to 9am: deli (lunch) -
